_doc: Shared-AcroForm-field split (correctness). The single AcroForm field `Town` has 7 kid widgets reused across two different captions: the court-location caption on pp.4/7/11/12/15 ("COURT LOCATION (where you are filing this action)" / "DISTRICT COURT Location (Town)" / "Court Location (Town)") AND the "Defendant/Respondent Location (Town)" caption on pp.13/14. mapping.json maps `town` -> `matter.court_location`, so the courthouse town legitimately fills the 5 court-location boxes but FANS OUT onto the 2 respondent-location boxes, stamping the courthouse town as the respondent's residence town (a false statement). The two respondent appearances (pp.13/14) are detached into their own terminal fields and blanked (clear:true) so the courthouse town stops fanning onto them; the 5 court-location widgets still receive the value. Verified via rendered text-in-rect on the filled sample (court_location='Portland'): before the split Portland printed in the pp.13/14 respondent boxes; after, those boxes are blank while pp.4/7/11/12/15 still show Portland. Re-fill is not supported (clear-only) and the respondent's town is not a canonical fact here, so blank is the correct outcome (better blank than the other appearance's wrong value) — this removes a false statement, it does not leave a fillable fact unfilled. The sibling `County` AcroForm field (widgets on pp.11/13/14) is currently UNMAPPED and renders blank everywhere, so it has no live defect and needs no split.

splits._why: Town widget on pg13 is the "Defendant/Respondent Location (Town)" caption, NOT the court-location caption of the other 5 pages (pp.4/7/11/12/15); detached + blanked so matter.court_location stops fanning onto the respondent's residence town.
Same "Defendant/Respondent Location (Town)" caption as pg13 (this packet repeats the caption block); detached + blanked for the same reason.
